prompt: What is the main goal of Week 3 in this course?
tags: Unit 3 Overview: Fine-Tuning Your First LLM End-to-End
theory

options.text: To learn about different LLM architectures
options.feedback: Incorrect: Week 3 focuses on practical fine-tuning, not learning architectures.

options.text: To take a base model, fine-tune it on a real dataset, and measure improvement
options.feedback: Correct: Week 3 is about hands-on fine-tuning with real datasets and measurable improvements.

options.text: To compare different pre-training strategies
options.feedback: Incorrect: The focus is on fine-tuning, not pre-training.

options.text: To study theoretical aspects of transformer models
options.feedback: Incorrect: This week emphasizes practical application over theory.

prompt: What dataset will be used for fine-tuning in Week 3?
tags: Unit 3 Overview: Fine-Tuning Your First LLM End-to-End
theory

options.text: GSM8K - a math reasoning dataset
options.feedback: Incorrect: GSM8K is mentioned as an example that required more than simple supervised fine-tuning.

options.text: SAMSum - a dataset of messenger-like dialogues with summaries
options.feedback: Correct: SAMSum is the main dataset used for the conversation summarization task.

options.text: SQuAD - a question answering dataset
options.feedback: Incorrect: SQuAD is not mentioned in this lesson.

options.text: Common Crawl - a web text dataset
options.feedback: Incorrect: Common Crawl is not the dataset being used for fine-tuning.

prompt: Why is SAMSum considered perfect for fine-tuning?
tags: Unit 3 Overview: Fine-Tuning Your First LLM End-to-End
theory

options.text: It's realistic and mirrors real conversation patterns
options.feedback: Correct: SAMSum contains messenger-like dialogues similar to real chat or customer support scenarios.

options.text: It's compact for fast training but large enough to show improvement
options.feedback: Correct: The dataset size allows for quick fine-tuning runs while still demonstrating measurable improvements.

options.text: It's evaluated with ROUGE, a standard summarization metric
options.feedback: Correct: ROUGE provides a standardized way to measure summarization quality.

options.text: It contains millions of examples for deep learning
options.feedback: Incorrect: SAMSum is described as compact, not containing millions of examples.

prompt: What are the three key shifts mentioned for Week 3?
tags: Unit 3 Overview: Fine-Tuning Your First LLM End-to-End
theory

options.text: From theory to practice
options.feedback: Correct: Students will actually perform fine-tuning instead of just learning about it.

options.text: From notebooks to infrastructure
options.feedback: Correct: The progression moves from Colab to cloud GPUs and config-driven pipelines.

options.text: From 'it works' to 'it's reproducible'
options.feedback: Correct: Focus shifts to experiment tracking, versioning, and documentation.

options.text: From single-task to multi-task learning
options.feedback: Incorrect: The focus remains on the single task of conversation summarization.

prompt: What common issues might students face during Week 3?
tags: Unit 3 Overview: Fine-Tuning Your First LLM End-to-End
theory

options.text: CUDA out-of-memory issues
options.feedback: Correct: GPU memory limitations are a common challenge in LLM fine-tuning.

options.text: Library version mismatches
options.feedback: Correct: Dependency conflicts are frequent in ML environments.

options.text: YAML misconfigurations
options.feedback: Correct: Configuration errors are common when setting up training pipelines.

options.text: RunPod setup quirks or authentication hiccups
options.feedback: Correct: Cloud platform setup often has initial authentication and configuration challenges.

prompt: What is the recommended approach when facing errors during Week 3?
tags: Unit 3 Overview: Fine-Tuning Your First LLM End-to-End
theory

options.text: Skip the problematic part and move on
options.feedback: Incorrect: The lesson emphasizes working through errors as part of the learning process.

options.text: Slow down, read the error carefully, and use it as a guide
options.feedback: Correct: Troubleshooting is presented as a core skill that deepens understanding.

options.text: Immediately ask for help from forums
options.feedback: Incorrect: While forums can help, the first step should be understanding the error.

options.text: Restart the entire environment
options.feedback: Incorrect: Restarting without understanding the error won't build troubleshooting skills.

prompt: What is the first step in Lesson 1?
tags: Unit 3 Overview: Fine-Tuning Your First LLM End-to-End
theory

options.text: Fine-tune the model with LoRA
options.feedback: Incorrect: Fine-tuning comes after establishing the baseline.

options.text: Set up RunPod cloud GPUs
options.feedback: Incorrect: RunPod setup is covered in Lesson 4.

options.text: Load Llama 3.2 1B and evaluate it on SAMSum to establish baseline
options.feedback: Correct: Establishing baseline performance is the starting point before any fine-tuning.

options.text: Integrate Weights & Biases for tracking
options.feedback: Incorrect: W&B integration is introduced in Lesson 5.

prompt: What metric will be used to measure summarization performance?
tags: Unit 3 Overview: Fine-Tuning Your First LLM End-to-End
theory

options.text: BLEU score
options.feedback: Incorrect: BLEU is used for translation, not mentioned for this task.

options.text: ROUGE score
options.feedback: Correct: ROUGE is explicitly mentioned as the standard summarization metric.

options.text: Perplexity
options.feedback: Incorrect: Perplexity measures language model quality, not summarization specifically.

options.text: Accuracy
options.feedback: Incorrect: Accuracy is not the appropriate metric for summarization tasks.

prompt: What tools and infrastructure will be used throughout Week 3?
tags: Unit 3 Overview: Fine-Tuning Your First LLM End-to-End
theory

options.text: Google Colab for initial prototyping
options.feedback: Correct: The week starts simple in Colab before scaling to cloud GPUs.

options.text: RunPod cloud GPUs for scaling
options.feedback: Correct: RunPod is introduced for faster training with fewer resource limits.

options.text: Weights & Biases for experiment tracking
options.feedback: Correct: W&B is integrated for reproducibility and metric logging.

options.text: Config-driven pipelines for reproducibility
options.feedback: Correct: Configuration files are used to make experiments reproducible.

prompt: What base model is mentioned for starting the fine-tuning process?
tags: Unit 3 Overview: Fine-Tuning Your First LLM End-to-End
theory

options.text: GPT-4
options.feedback: Incorrect: GPT-4 is not mentioned as the base model for this exercise.

options.text: Llama 3.2 1B
options.feedback: Correct: Llama 3.2 1B is specifically mentioned as the starting base model.

options.text: BERT
options.feedback: Incorrect: BERT is not suitable for generative summarization tasks.

options.text: T5
options.feedback: Incorrect: T5 is not mentioned as the model for this week's exercises.

prompt: What makes Week 3 different from previous weeks according to the content?
tags: Unit 3 Overview: Fine-Tuning Your First LLM End-to-End
theory

options.text: It focuses more on mathematical foundations
options.feedback: Incorrect: Week 3 is described as the most hands-on week, not theoretical.

options.text: It's the most hands-on week with real fine-tuning
options.feedback: Correct: The content explicitly states this is the most hands-on week yet.

options.text: It covers only theoretical concepts
options.feedback: Incorrect: The emphasis is on practical application over theory.

options.text: It requires no coding
options.feedback: Incorrect: This week involves extensive coding and troubleshooting.

prompt: What is the purpose of establishing a baseline before fine-tuning?
tags: Unit 3 Overview: Fine-Tuning Your First LLM End-to-End
theory

options.text: To waste time before starting real work
options.feedback: Incorrect: Baseline evaluation is crucial for measuring improvement.

options.text: To have a reference point for measuring improvements
options.feedback: Correct: The baseline provides the starting point to quantify fine-tuning benefits.

options.text: To test if the model can load correctly
options.feedback: Incorrect: While loading is tested, the main purpose is measurement.

options.text: To compare with other students' results
options.feedback: Incorrect: The baseline is for measuring your own model's improvement.

prompt: What does the course say about troubleshooting and errors?
tags: Unit 3 Overview: Fine-Tuning Your First LLM End-to-End
theory

options.text: Errors should be avoided at all costs
options.feedback: Incorrect: Errors are presented as normal and part of the learning process.

options.text: Troubleshooting is core to LLM engineering
options.feedback: Correct: The content emphasizes that each fix deepens understanding.

options.text: Errors indicate you're doing something wrong
options.feedback: Incorrect: Errors are normal and expected in ML engineering.

options.text: You should immediately switch to an easier task
options.feedback: Incorrect: Persistence through errors is encouraged as a learning opportunity.

prompt: What will students have by the end of Week 3?
tags: Unit 3 Overview: Fine-Tuning Your First LLM End-to-End
theory

options.text: A theoretical understanding of fine-tuning
options.feedback: Incorrect: The outcome is practical, not just theoretical.

options.text: A complete, reproducible fine-tuning pipeline
options.feedback: Correct: The goal is a reusable pipeline for any model or task.

options.text: A published research paper
options.feedback: Incorrect: The focus is on practical skills, not publication.

options.text: A job at an ML company
options.feedback: Incorrect: While skills are valuable, employment is not guaranteed.

prompt: Why is GSM8K mentioned in the lesson?
tags: Unit 3 Overview: Fine-Tuning Your First LLM End-to-End
theory

options.text: As the main dataset for the week
options.feedback: Incorrect: SAMSum is the main dataset, not GSM8K.

options.text: As an example of a task requiring more than simple supervised fine-tuning
options.feedback: Correct: GSM8K is mentioned to show that some tasks need advanced techniques.

options.text: As a backup dataset if SAMSum doesn't work
options.feedback: Incorrect: GSM8K is not presented as an alternative dataset.

options.text: As a dataset for evaluation only
options.feedback: Incorrect: It's mentioned as an example of a challenging task, not for evaluation.

prompt: What professional habits will students develop in Week 3?
tags: Unit 3 Overview: Fine-Tuning Your First LLM End-to-End
theory

options.text: Writing documentation and reports
options.feedback: Incorrect: While important, this isn't the main focus mentioned.

options.text: Tracking experiments, versioning results, and documenting process
options.feedback: Correct: These are explicitly mentioned as habits of professional ML engineers.

options.text: Attending team meetings and standups
options.feedback: Incorrect: The focus is on technical practices, not meetings.

options.text: Code review and pull request processes
options.feedback: Incorrect: While valuable, these aren't the highlighted habits for this week.

prompt: What is the progression path for the week's lessons?
tags: Unit 3 Overview: Fine-Tuning Your First LLM End-to-End
theory

options.text: Start with cloud, then move to local
options.feedback: Incorrect: The progression is from local/simple to cloud/advanced.

options.text: Baseline → Managed fine-tuning → Self-hosted fine-tuning → Cloud scaling → Tracking
options.feedback: Correct: This represents the logical progression from simple to advanced workflows.

options.text: Theory → Practice → Deployment → Maintenance
options.feedback: Incorrect: The focus is on different fine-tuning approaches, not deployment lifecycle.

options.text: Small model → Large model → Ensemble → Distillation
options.feedback: Incorrect: The model size remains consistent; focus is on methodology.
